title: commelinaceae
intro: The Commelinaceae rank, also known as the Spiderwort family, is a family of flowering plants that belongs to the monocots. It is defined as a family of plants that includes around 800 species, characterized by their small to medium-sized flowers, often with three petals and a distinctive shape. Members of the Commelinaceae family are generally found in tropical and subtropical regions, with a few species also occurring in temperate areas. This family includes a diverse range of plants, such as spiderworts, dayflowers, and wavy leaved grasses, and they can be found growing in a variety of environments, from wetlands to dry scrublands.
